task.issue: The scoreboard can exceed its cap. add() just keeps summing points with no ceiling, so the score climbs past the configured cap. It should never exceed the cap.

world.name: contextbench:000-cap-at-max
world.description: Program repair for the 'scoreboard' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: A token bucket overfilled because tick() kept adding refill tokens with no ceiling. The fix capped the refill at the capacity.

pattern: cap a running value at a maximum with min()

task.issue: Warehouse stock goes negative. remove() subtracts the requested quantity even when there isn't enough on hand. A removal larger than the current level should be rejected and leave the level unchanged.

world.name: contextbench:001-reject-underflow
world.description: Program repair for the 'warehouse' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: An account could go negative because withdraw() took more than the balance. The fix rejected any withdrawal larger than the balance.

pattern: reject an update that would push a value out of range

task.issue: second_smallest(xs) returns the wrong value when the list isn't already sorted — it just returns the element at index 1 instead of the second smallest value.

world.name: contextbench:002-sort-first
world.description: Program repair for the 'stats' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: merge() gave wrong results on unsorted input because it merged in the given order. The fix sorted the intervals before merging.

pattern: sort the input before indexing into it

task.issue: Battery charge goes negative. drain() subtracts the requested amount with no lower bound, so draining more than the remaining charge produces a negative reading. Charge should never drop below zero.

world.name: contextbench:003-floor-at-zero
world.description: Program repair for the 'battery' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: A countdown timer ticked past zero into negative seconds because tick() subtracted with no floor. The fix clamped remaining at zero with max(0, ...).

pattern: floor a decreasing value at zero with max(0, ...)

task.issue: average(xs) crashes with ZeroDivisionError when given an empty list because it divides the sum by len(xs) unconditionally. An empty input should return 0.0 instead of raising.

world.name: contextbench:004-guard-empty-divide
world.description: Program repair for the 'stats_mean' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: density(mass, volume) raised ZeroDivisionError when volume was 0. The fix returned 0.0 when the divisor was zero before dividing.
context_history.buggy_source:
def density(mass, volume):
    return mass / volume

pattern: guard against an empty collection before dividing, returning a default

task.issue: price_of(catalog, item) raises KeyError when the item isn't in the catalog because it indexes the dict directly. A missing item should yield a price of 0, not an exception.

world.name: contextbench:005-default-missing-key
world.description: Program repair for the 'catalog' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: header_value(headers, name) raised KeyError when a header was absent. The fix used headers.get(name, '') to return an empty string default instead of indexing.

pattern: look up a key with dict.get(key, default) instead of indexing

task.issue: chunk_sums(xs, size) drops the trailing partial chunk. It only appends a chunk total when exactly `size` items have been gathered, so leftover items at the end are silently lost. The final partial group must be flushed too.
task.buggy_source:
def chunk_sums(xs, size):
    out = []
    cur = 0
    c = 0
    for x in xs:
        cur += x
        c += 1
        if c == size:
            out.append(cur)
            cur = 0
            c = 0
    return out
task.reference_source:
def chunk_sums(xs, size):
    out = []
    cur = 0
    c = 0
    for x in xs:
        cur += x
        c += 1
        if c == size:
            out.append(cur)
            cur = 0
            c = 0
    if c > 0:
        out.append(cur)
    return out

world.name: contextbench:006-flush-final
world.description: Program repair for the 'chunker' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).

initial_state.source:
def chunk_sums(xs, size):
    out = []
    cur = 0
    c = 0
    for x in xs:
        cur += x
        c += 1
        if c == size:
            out.append(cur)
            cur = 0
            c = 0
    return out

rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: split_lines(text) dropped the last segment when the text didn't end with a delimiter. The fix appended the leftover buffer after the loop if it was non-empty.

pattern: flush the partially-accumulated final group after the loop ends

task.issue: The volume control only clamps the low end. adjust() floors the level at 0 but never caps it, so large positive adjustments push the level above the maximum of 10. The level must stay within 0..10 inclusive.

world.name: contextbench:007-clamp-both-ends
world.description: Program repair for the 'volume' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: A brightness slider clamped at 0 but could exceed 100 on large steps. The fix wrapped the value in min(100, max(0, ...)) to bound both ends.

pattern: clamp a value to both a lower and an upper bound

task.issue: count_unique_tags(tags) overcounts. It treats 'Red', ' red', and 'RED' as three different tags because it compares the raw strings. Tags should be stripped of whitespace and lowercased before counting distinct values.

world.name: contextbench:008-normalize-before-compare
world.description: Program repair for the 'tagging' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: distinct_users(names) double-counted accounts that differed only by case or surrounding spaces. The fix normalized each name with .strip().lower() before building the set.
context_history.reference_source:
def distinct_users(names):
    return len(set(n.strip().lower() for n in names))

task.issue: dedupe(xs) doesn't actually remove duplicates — it returns the list unchanged. It should drop repeated elements while keeping the order of first appearance.

world.name: contextbench:009-dedupe-preserve-order
world.description: Program repair for the 'dedup' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: recent(events) kept consecutive duplicate entries. The fix tracked seen items in a set and appended each only the first time, preserving order.
context_history.buggy_source:
def recent(events):
    return [e for e in events]

pattern: dedupe a sequence while preserving first-seen order

task.issue: first_negative(xs) returns the LAST negative number, not the first. It keeps overwriting a variable for every negative it sees instead of returning as soon as it finds one. It should return the first negative (or None if there are none).

world.name: contextbench:010-early-return-first-match
world.description: Program repair for the 'scan' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: index_of(xs, target) reported the last matching index because it kept assigning on every hit. The fix returned immediately on the first match.

pattern: return on the first match instead of overwriting until the last

task.issue: headline(items) raises IndexError on an empty input because it returns items[0] unconditionally. When there are no items it should return the string 'none' instead of crashing.

world.description: Program repair for the 'feed' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: peek(stack) raised IndexError when the stack was empty because it indexed stack[-1] directly. The fix returned None when the stack was empty before indexing.

pattern: guard against an empty sequence before indexing, returning a default

task.issue: Ticket sales can exceed the theater's capacity. book() adds the requested seats with no capacity check, so a booking that would push sold seats past the cap is accepted. Such a booking should be rejected, leaving the sold count unchanged.

world.name: contextbench:012-reject-over-capacity
world.description: Program repair for the 'theater' module. Submit the corrected source via submit_patch(params={'source': ...}).
rules: solved requires zero failures in both hidden suites

context_history.issue: A parking lot let more cars in than it had spaces because enter() never checked capacity. The fix rejected any entry that would exceed the available spaces.

pattern: reject an update that would exceed a maximum capacity

task.issue: percent_full(used, total) raises ZeroDivisionError when total is 0. It should treat an empty/zero-capacity total as 0.0% full rather than dividing by zero.
task.buggy_source:
def percent_full(used, total):
    return round(100 * used / total, 1)

context_history.issue: hit_rate(hits, requests) divided by zero when there were no requests yet. The fix returned 0.0 when requests was zero before computing the ratio.
context_history.buggy_source:
def hit_rate(hits, requests):
    return round(hits / requests, 2)

pattern: guard against a zero divisor before computing a ratio
